name: Fullscreen Presentation-Surface Intent
summary: A surface that takes over the whole display for an immersive, distraction-free presentation — the Fullscreen API to fill the viewport, cursor auto-hide after idle, and a Screen Wake Lock so the screen does not sleep. General, not deck-specific: a video player, an image lightbox, a kiosk app, and a slide deck all want the same capability. Owns the presentation-surface concern; what plays inside it (slides, video) is the consumer's. Homed in webportals per the ratified distributed placement (#1175) — it is a surface/portal capability, not a deck contract.

scope.description: What goes fullscreen. element = a specific surface element fills the screen (the deck/video container — the default, keeps the rest of the app addressable); document = the whole document element.

cursor.description: Pointer behavior in fullscreen. auto-hide = the cursor hides after a short idle and reappears on movement (the presentation default); visible = the cursor always shows (when controls must stay discoverable).

wakeLock.description: Whether a Screen Wake Lock is held while the surface is fullscreen. held = keep the display awake (presentation/kiosk); none = allow system sleep per policy.

description:
An immersive presentation surface

Going fullscreen for a presentation is more than the Fullscreen API: the pointer should fade when idle, and the screen must not sleep mid-talk. The Fullscreen intent bundles those three platform concerns — Fullscreen API, cursor auto-hide, Screen Wake Lock — into one declarable surface capability.

General, not deck-specific

A slide deck wants it, but so do a video player, an image lightbox, and a kiosk app — which is why it homes in Web Portals as a surface capability rather than in a deck contract (it patches a coverage gap the deck survey first surfaced). It owns the surface; the content inside is the consumer's concern.

Platform notes

  • User gesture: entering fullscreen requires a user activation (the API rejects programmatic entry) — the intent is declared, but armed by a gesture.
  • Wake Lock lifecycle: the lock is released automatically on exit / tab-hide and must be re-requested on return.
